Who I Am

Professional Summary

Construction engineer with 3+ years of hands-on experience on $870M+ building programs in the United States and India. Skilled in RFI management, shop drawing coordination, change order processing, contract document control, and construction scheduling. Experienced working across all levels of project teams, including subcontractors, vendors, and field crews. Holds an M.S. in Construction Management from New York University — completed May 2026 — with strong proficiency in Primavera P6, MS Project, Procore, Bluebeam, and Microsoft Office Suite.

Career Timeline

Professional Experience

Engineta LLCMay 2025 – May 2026
VDC Engineer
JFK $19B Redevelopment — T1 & T6 · Barclays Center Phase 2 · New York, NY
  • Conducted field walkthroughs with project managers to document line-out items, cost tracking, and progress verification across multiple active construction sites.
  • Facilitated communication between vendors, contractors, and field teams to address project challenges and maintain workflow continuity.
  • Leveraged Bluebeam and MS Excel for weekly progress analysis, identifying bottlenecks and implementing recovery strategies.
  • Assisted in managing 50+ carpenters weekly, ensuring compliance with contract drawings and client specifications.
  • Resolved 4+ RFIs per week, preventing project delays and managing change order impacts.
New York UniversitySep 2025 – May 2026
Graduate Teaching Assistant
Geotechnical Engineering Laboratory · Brooklyn, NY
  • Conducted geotechnical engineering laboratory experiments supporting undergraduate and doctoral research programs.
  • Trained students in laboratory testing procedures, engineering analysis, and safety protocols.
  • Prepared technical documentation summarizing experimental findings and engineering analysis results.
L&T ConstructionOct 2022 – Jun 2024
Project Engineer
MAHSR C6 — $870M Mumbai–Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail · Vadodara, India
  • Monitored construction progress against baseline schedules and coordinated manpower, equipment, and material planning.
  • Maintained project schedules and activity sequencing using Primavera P6.
  • Engaged trade partners, subcontractors, and stakeholders to gather field insights and drive on-site resolutions.
  • Contributed to execution plan improvements that reduced activity timelines by 3%, earning the Youth Icon Award (2023).
